text: You find Grubwin on the rooftop after school. The door was unlocked, like he said. He's sitting on the low wall, looking at the city, clipboard on his knees with nothing written on it.
He doesn't seem surprised you came up.
You sit beside him. The city hums. There are clouds doing something interesting.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I've been thinking about Vermia's thing. The second hymn. What you do after.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I think she means something more than Scatha. I think she means — when you've wanted something to be real for so long, and then something happens that might count as real, even a little — what do you do with that?

speaker: Grubwin
text: Do you keep wanting more? Do you let it be enough? Do you pretend you weren't hoping?

speaker: Grubwin
text: All three, at different times. That's why she needs to write a song about it.

speaker: Grubwin
text: I'm glad you found us, [player]. The club. I know I don't say things like that.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Not enough. You changed the energy a bit. Good way.
